_comment: Printed-arithmetic computed fields consumed by maine_forms_engine.computations. Every formula_text is the instruction printed verbatim on the blank (tests/test_computations.py re-checks this against the fetched PDF). Warnings-only: a supplied value is always written as-is. Deliberately NOT declared: line 13 (conditional: 'Add lines 9c, 10, 11 and 12; if biweekly, multiply x 2'), line 7c's '(Add lines 7a and 7b.)' (the form's line-7 widgets are both labeled 'b.'; no mapped 7a input exists), and lines 14a/14b obligations — the blank prints '(Multiply line 8b by line 13)' under BOTH Parent 1's and Parent 2's obligation, and the line-8 share boxes are percentage entries ('a. %' / 'b. %', now mapped as facts.parent1_share_of_adjusted_income / facts.parent2_share_of_adjusted_income): the divide-by-100 a percent implies is not a printed literal, so the product is not expressible without inventing a constant.

facts.basic_weekly_support_total.formula_text: Total number of children (a) multiplied by amount from table (b) = 9c.
facts.basic_weekly_support_total.note: the printed line carries fill-in blanks between (a), (b) and 9c; facts.amount_from_table is a SUPPLIED fact — the filer reads it off the printed Child Support Table per page 3 ('CALCULATING “AMOUNT FROM TABLE” FOR LINE 9 OF THE WORKSHEET'); the statutory table itself is never embedded or computed

facts.total_weekly_health_insurance_cost.note: the printed Total at the foot of the '10. Weekly health insurance cost for children' Name & amount-per-child-per-week column

facts.child_care_total.note: the printed Total at the foot of the '11. Weekly child care expenses' Name & amount-per-child-per-week column

facts.medical_expense_total.note: the printed Total at the foot of the '12. Extraordinary medical expenses' Name & amount-per-child-per-week column

facts.parent1_pays_as_support.formula_text: Non-Primary Care Provider Adjustments (Amounts paid directly by Parent 1) Weekly health insurance (line 10) - $ Weekly child care (line 11) - $ Extraordinary Medical Expenses (line 12) - $ Parent 1 pays as support = $
facts.parent1_pays_as_support.note: line 14a: the printed '-' adjustments subtract from Parent 1's support obligation down to the printed '=' result

facts.parent2_pays_as_support.formula_text: Non-Primary Care Provider Adjustments (Amounts paid directly by Parent 2) Weekly health insurance (line 10) - $ Weekly child care (line 11) - $ Extraordinary Medical Expenses (line 12) - $ Parent 2 pays as support = $
facts.parent2_pays_as_support.note: line 14b: the printed '-' adjustments subtract from Parent 2's support obligation down to the printed '=' result
